title: croton velleriflorus
intro: Croton velleriflorus, also known as the "gurhal" or "gurhal tree," is a deciduous tree species native to the tropical regions of the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Croton velleriflorus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. The plant is typically prepared as a decoction, powder, or ointment, and is also used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine systems.
